title: ranunculus sulphureus subsp. exaltatus
intro: Ranunculus sulphureus subsp. exaltatus, also known as the tall buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant within the Ranunculus sulphureus species, characterized by its bright yellow flowers with five petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus sulphureus subsp. exaltatus are thought to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis, and soothing skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
